general_goal_of_analysis: Theoretical rethinking of International Relations from a Neo-Gramscian Critical Theory perspective, analyzing US hegemony and consensus building in Latin America through the Atlas Network
brief_justification: This is a theoretical paper grounded in Neo-Gramscian Critical Theory that examines US hegemony and consensus fabrication in Latin America through the Atlas Network. It relies on primary documents, public records (e.g., Atlas Network Form 990 filings), and specialized bibliography. It contains no quantitative analysis, no statistical tables, no figures, and no equations. The paper is fundamentally a qualitative-theoretical contribution to International Relations, not an empirical quantitative study.
